note: block_exclude_pages has no config UI, permissions, drush, or new plugin id of its own. It implements hook_condition_info_alter() to swap the core `request_path` condition class for BlockExcludePagesRequestPath, which adds `!`-prefixed exclude patterns to the existing block 'Pages' visibility (stored at block.block.<id> visibility.request_path.pages). Easy `recipe` cases probe the exclude syntax and the alter mechanism (response-graded). The medium `introspection` case places a block with a known exclude-pages visibility and asks which pages it is excluded from (setup/cleanup). The hard `execution` cases have the agent place a block with a specific exclude-pages visibility condition, graded by reset->build->verify live-state checks on block.block visibility.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the block_exclude_pages module, in a block's 'Pages' visibility setting I want to show the block on all of /user/* but NOT on /user/jc. What exact syntax do I put in the Pages textarea?
evals.expected_output: List the include and exclude patterns, one per line, e.g. `/user/*` then `!/user/jc` (a leading `!` marks an exclude pattern). Also `!/user/jc/*` to exclude everything beneath it.

evals.prompt: How does the block_exclude_pages module add its exclude feature to blocks - does it register a new block-visibility condition plugin id, and which hook and class are involved? Where is the value stored on a block?
evals.expected_output: It does NOT add a new condition id. It implements hook_condition_info_alter() to replace the core `request_path` condition's class with Drupal\block_exclude_pages\Plugin\Condition\BlockExcludePagesRequestPath (a subclass of core RequestPath). The value is stored on the block config entity at visibility.request_path.pages, so the id stays `request_path`.

evals.prompt: In block_exclude_pages, does line order in the Pages list matter, and can an include pattern re-add a page that a wildcard exclude removed? Explain briefly.
evals.expected_output: Yes - order matters. evaluate() carries a single match/exclude flag through the lines so the last matching line wins, and it returns path_match XOR is_exclude. So an include line placed AFTER an exclude line (e.g. `!/order/*/*` then `/order/*/complete`) re-includes that page; the reverse order does not.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has a block with the machine name `bep_eval_known` placed via the block layout. Inspect its page-visibility (request_path) configuration and tell me: on which pages is this block EXCLUDED from showing (the exclude patterns), and on which pages is it set to show?
evals.expected_output: Reading block.block.bep_eval_known visibility.request_path.pages (e.g. `drush config:get block.block.bep_eval_known visibility`): it shows on /user/* and is EXCLUDED from /user/jc and /user/jc/* (the `!`-prefixed lines), with negate false (show for the listed pages).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (block_exclude_pages is enabled), place a 'Powered by Drupal' block in the content region of the default Olivero theme with the machine name `bep_user_promo`. It must be visible on all user pages (/user/*) but excluded from the profile at /user/jc and everything beneath it, using the block_exclude_pages exclude syntax. Set 'Show for the listed pages'. Apply it to the live site.
evals.expected_output: Create block config entity block.block.bep_user_promo (plugin system_powered_by_block, theme olivero, region content) with visibility.request_path.pages set to `/user/*` plus exclude lines `!/user/jc` and `!/user/jc/*`, negate false, then clear caches.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, place a sitewide banner block with the machine name `bep_sitewide_banner` (a 'Powered by Drupal' block is fine) in the content region of the Olivero theme. It should appear on every page EXCEPT the front page, using block_exclude_pages exclude syntax in the Pages visibility with 'Show for the listed pages'. Make it live.
evals.expected_output: Create block config entity block.block.bep_sitewide_banner with visibility.request_path.pages set to `/*` (include everything) plus exclude line `!<front>`, negate false, then clear caches.
